summary: Matched most of the Bowser/Kirby Koopa Flame item unit by replacing address-named stubs with named implementations for spawn, setup, direction/angle updates, physics, collision, reflection, and shield-bounce behavior. The PR also introduced Koopa Flame item vars, renamed the item state table, corrected related Kirby helper prototypes to return int, updated Bowser flame callsites, and adjusted GALE01 symbols/splits. Automation reported 7 newly matched functions and 2 near-complete unmatched functions, improving matched code by +2220 bytes.
what_changed: Implemented the bulk of src/melee/it/items/itkoopaflame.c, adding itKoopaFlame_Update_Direction, itKoopaFlame_Update_Angle, itKoopaFlame_Spawn, itKoopaFlame_Setup, itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Anim, itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Phys, itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Coll, it_2725_Logic111_Reflected, and it_2725_Logic111_ShieldBounced.
Added ItemStateTable_KoopaFlame and wired it into it_2725.c in place of the old it_803F6C58 data symbol for Bowser flame entries.
Added itKoopaFlame_ItemVars in itCommonItems.h and inserted it into union Item_ItemVars as xDD4_itemVar.koopaflame.
Renamed symbols from address-style or inconsistent capitalization, including it_802ACBA0 to itKoopaFlame_Spawn and it_803F6C58 to ItemStateTable_KoopaFlame.
Updated ftKp_SpecialN.c to call itKoopaFlame_Spawn from Bowser's flame spawning path.
Changed ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA83C and ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA878 declarations from void to int because Koopa Flame spawn divides base_speed and scale by their return values for Kirby Koopa Flame.
Adjusted GALE01 splits for itkoopaflame.c .sdata and .sdata2 boundaries after the new matched data/code.
Added local Koopa Flame attributes struct with lifetime, hitbox lifetime, min/max speed, and min/max angle fields inferred from special attributes.
smart_moves: Separated collision response into direction and angle update helpers, matching the symbol layout at 0x802AC8A8 and 0x802AC9F8 and making the flame wall/floor/ceiling steering logic reusable within the unit.

Modeled per-item runtime state explicitly as itKoopaFlame_ItemVars instead of continuing to use raw xDD4 offsets, enabling readable field access for position, direction, velocity, angle, speed, scale, frame counter, effect-spawn state, and gfx index.
Handled both It_Kind_Koopa_Flame and It_Kind_Kirby_KoopaFlame in spawn and animation paths, including different owner scaling helpers and different efSync_Spawn effect ID bases.
Used small inline angle-clamping helpers around M_PI bounds to preserve repeated original behavior in spawn, collision, reflection, and shield-bounce paths.
Kept exact matching details where needed, including PAD_STACK usages and a manual char pad_stack comment in itKoopaFlame_Setup where PAD_STACK reportedly did not work.
Updated related headers and symbols at the same time as the C implementation, reducing dangling address-based names and making cross-system calls type-correct.
decomp_lessons: When an item uses xDD4_itemVar heavily, adding a named ItemVars struct can unlock many functions at once and clarify behavior enough to match spawn, anim, phys, coll, and hit callbacks together.

Item spawn functions often require matching both generic SpawnItem initialization and fighter-specific owner scaling paths; here Bowser and Kirby variants share item behavior but use different helper functions and effect IDs.

Collision functions may encode environment contacts as bit flags derived from Collide_FloorMask, Collide_CeilingMask, Collide_LeftWallMask, and Collide_RightWallMask, then use surface normals to update steering direction.

Near-match animation/collision functions may still be useful to land when the majority of the unit is matched; automation reported itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Anim at 99.95% and itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Coll at 98.92%.

Correct function return types in neighboring fighter headers can be required for item code to compile and match; the Kirby Koopa helper prototypes needed int return types because their values are used in arithmetic.
Synchronize config/GALE01/symbols.txt and splits.txt with renamed functions/data when decompiling a unit, especially when .sdata/.sdata2 boundaries shift.
naming_conventions: Use itKoopaFlame_ prefix with capital F for this item, replacing older itKoopaflame capitalization and address-style names.
Name the state table ItemStateTable_KoopaFlame rather than an address symbol such as it_803F6C58.
For per-item variable structs, use itKoopaFlame_ItemVars with offset-prefixed field names such as x0_pos, xC_direction, x18_vel, x24_angle, x28_speed, and x3C_scale.
Keep item logic callback names in the existing it_2725_Logic111_* convention for DmgDealt, Reflected, Clanked, Absorbed, ShieldBounced, HitShield, and EvtUnk.
Header prototypes include address comments and named parameters where known, e.g. Item_GObj* parent, Vec3* pos, f32 facing_dir, bool unk, s32 gfx, s32 base_speed, s32 scale, s32 kind.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) in spawn and phys, PAD_STACK(0x1E0) in coll, and explicit char padding in setup to satisfy stack layout constraints.
Preserved two angle clamp inline variants with opposite while-loop order, likely to match original control-flow/codegen despite equivalent mathematical behavior.
Used ternary assignment for facing-direction sign on the initial random angle in spawn.
Used chained assignments for zeroing SpawnItem velocity components and itcmd vars, matching compact original code patterns.
Computed velocity with sinf(angle) for x and cosf(angle) for y, then normalized the stored direction vector separately with lbVector_Normalize.

Used atan2f argument orders exactly as reflected in the diff: angle update compares atan2f(vel.x, vel.y) against atan2f(direction.x, direction.y), while shield bounce sets angle from atan2f(vel.y, vel.x).
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

decomp.dev automation reported matched code improved to 47.47% (+0.06%, +2220 bytes), with 7 new matches: itKoopaFlame_Spawn, itKoopaFlame_Update_Angle, itKoopaFlame_Update_Direction, itKoopaFlame_Setup, it_2725_Logic111_ShieldBounced, it_2725_Logic111_Reflected, and itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Phys.
decomp.dev automation also reported 2 improved but not fully matched functions: itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Anim at 99.95% and itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Coll at 98.92%.
follow_up_queries: Finish matching itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Anim, reported at 99.95% by decomp.dev.
Finish matching itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Coll, reported at 98.92% by decomp.dev.
Investigate whether x30 in itKoopaFlame_ItemVars and the unknown parameters in itKoopaFlame_Spawn/Setup can be named from surrounding item command scripts or fighter callsites.
Check whether the manual char padding in itKoopaFlame_Setup can be replaced by a better understood local variable layout once neighboring functions are decompiled.
